column1: # Claim-discipline registry (bead mwm.9). One row per marketed capability.
#   readme_pattern     a substring that, if present in README.md, means the claim IS being made
#   capability_key     the key under capabilities --json .features that backs the claim
#   expected_substr    the capability value must CONTAIN this (proves the tool agrees)
#   proof_path         a repo file (gate script or test) that proves the capability
# A row is enforced only when its readme_pattern is present in the README, so the
# registry never forces a claim the README does not actually make.
